mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 4.0% signals tight labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and declining capacity utilization signals strong manufacturing activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative correlation with INDPRO growth when FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,200k units signals weak consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during recessionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances with HOUST below 1,200k during recessions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in CPI inflation above 2.5% triggers central bank tightening expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 50%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 50k indicates weak labor market performance, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances with PAYEMS below 50k.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal weakening demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show positive correlation with RSAFS declines when HOUST is above 1,200k.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 1.0% combined with rising capacity utilization signals strong supply-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative correlation with INDPRO growth when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.
